cells.docstring: Paper-faithful assembly space data `(Ω, U, J)`. This intentionally keeps `J` ternary (join predicate) as in the paper, rather than immediately choosing a graph encoding. cells.overlay_summary: Paper-faithful assembly space data `(Ω, U, J)`. This intentionally keeps `J` ternary (join predicate) as in the paper, rather than immediately choosing a graph encoding. The paper also defines `Ω` as the closure of `U` under `J`. We represent this closure property via the `closed` field in `AssemblySpace.Closed` (see `AssemblyPath.lean`).

cells.signature: Type u → Type u → Type u → Type u → Type u
cells.docstring: Kernel expression AST with a deliberately small 9-way constructor split. We parameterize universe levels separately from expression metavariables: - `Param` / `MetaLevel` appear inside `ULevel` values (Phase 7), - `MetaExpr` is used for expression metavariables. The chosen 9 constructors align with the “core” shapes used by kernels: `bvar`, `mvar`, `sort`, `const`, `app`, `lam`, `forallE`, `letE`, `lit`.
cells.overlay_summary: Kernel expression AST with a deliberately small 9-way constructor split. We parameterize universe levels separately from expression metavariables: - `Param` / `MetaLevel` appear inside `ULevel` values (Phase 7), - `MetaExpr` is used for expression metavariables. The chosen 9 constructors align with the “core” shapes used by kernels: `bvar`, `mvar`, `sort`, `const`, `app`, `lam`, `forallE`, `letE`, `lit`.

cells.docstring: A single R1CS constraint (A, B, C) meaning (A*x) * (B*x) = (C*x).
cells.overlay_summary: R1CS (Rank-1 Constraint System) constraints are quadratic equations of the form A*B=C where A,B,C are linear combinations. Any arithmetic circuit can be converted to R1CS form.

cells.signature: structure TV where T : Bool; F : Bool
cells.docstring: Truth values as 'is true' and 'is false' flags.
cells.overlay_summary: The LP (Logic of Paradox) truth value structure with independent 'is true' (T) and 'is false' (F) Boolean flags. This allows four combinations: true-only (T=1,F=0), false-only (T=0,F=1), both (T=1,F=1), and neither (T=0,F=0). The 'both' value is designated, enabling contradictions to be true.

cells.docstring: Orbital angular-momentum labels used in chemistry.

cells.docstring: Linear combinations over Q as a constant plus explicit terms.
cells.overlay_summary: Linear combinations are the building blocks of R1CS constraints. Each linear combination is a weighted sum of variables plus a constant term.

cells.docstring: `Loc K` is a “Grothendieck-style” total category of `K`-linear local systems over varying 1-type bases (groupoids). Objects are pairs `(X, V)` with `X : Grpd` and `V : X ⥤ Coeff K`. Morphisms `(X,V) ⟶ (Y,W)` are pairs `(f : X ⥤ Y, φ : V ⟶ f*W)` where `f*` is pullback by precomposition.
cells.overlay_summary: `Loc K` is a “Grothendieck-style” total category of `K`-linear local systems over varying 1-type bases (groupoids). Objects are pairs `(X, V)` with `X : Grpd` and `V : X ⥤ Coeff K`. Morphisms `(X,V) ⟶ (Y,W)` are pairs `(f : X ⥤ Y, φ : V ⟶ f*W)` where `f*` is pullback by precomposition.

cells.docstring: A key-bit comparison result.

cells.signature: Type u → Type u → Type u → Type (u + 1)
cells.docstring: A Mealy machine in `Para(Type)`: parameters `P` and a transition function `P × I × S → S × O`.
Norm witnesses for intensity vectors. The bounds are stored for future proofs about collapse and expand operators respecting the intended ℓ¹/ℓ² controls.
cells.overlay_summary: Norm witnesses for intensity vectors. The bounds are stored for future proofs about collapse and expand operators respecting the intended ℓ¹/ℓ² controls.

cells.signature: structure Rules (α : Type u) where compose : α → α → Finset α
cells.docstring: Binary assembly rule set returning the admissible composites for two parts.
cells.overlay_summary: Binary assembly rule set returning the admissible composites for two parts.

cells.docstring: Tiny alphabet of primitive parts for tests.
cells.overlay_summary: Tiny alphabet of primitive parts for tests.

cells.docstring: Per-lens completion/canonical-subset data, plus a truncation operator.
cells.overlay_summary: Per-lens completion/canonical-subset data, plus a truncation operator.

cells.docstring: A total store maps variable names to integers.
cells.overlay_summary: A total store maps variable names to integers.

cells.docstring: A subshell label `(n, l)` with principal quantum number `n` and orbital label `l`.
cells.overlay_summary: A subshell label `(n, l)` with principal quantum number `n` and orbital label `l`.

cells.signature: structure System where constraints : List Constraint
cells.docstring: An R1CS system is a finite list of constraints.
cells.overlay_summary: An R1CS system defines a computational relation: a witness satisfies the system iff it satisfies all constraints. This is the NPC language used in SNARK constructions.

cells.docstring: Eve's interception rate `p ∈ [0,1]`.
cells.overlay_summary: Eve's interception rate `p ∈ [0,1]`.

cells.docstring: Variables are de Bruijn-style indices.
cells.overlay_summary: Variables are de Bruijn-style indices.

cells.signature: (L : Type u) → (Ω : Type v) → [CompleteLattice L] → [CompleteLattice Ω] → Type (max u v)
cells.docstring: Strong hypothesis pack: `f` preserves binary meets.
cells.overlay_summary: Strong hypothesis pack: `f` preserves binary meets.

cells.docstring: Lightweight interface that any ZK backend can implement. It is intentionally generic and compiled from the existing BoolLens VM in this codebase.
cells.overlay_summary: Lightweight interface that any ZK backend can implement. It is intentionally generic and compiled from the existing BoolLens VM in this codebase.

cells.signature: {ι : Type u_1} → [Fintype ι] → [DecidableEq ι] → Matrix ι ι Complex → Prop
cells.docstring: Positive-semidefinite matrix predicate specialized to finite matrices.
cells.overlay_summary: Positive-semidefinite matrix predicate specialized to finite matrices.

cells.docstring: Substrate for the toy E91 formalization.
cells.overlay_summary: Substrate for the toy E91 formalization.

cells.signature: (C : Type u) → [inst : CategoryTheory.Category C] → [CategoryTheory.Limits.HasPullbacks C] → Type (max u v)
cells.docstring: `LocalOperator C` is a **universal closure operator** on subobjects: it consists of a closure operator on each `Subobject X`, stable under pullback. This is the “subobject-lattice” presentation of a Lawvere–Tierney modality. A full classifier-based presentation (endomorphism `j : Ω ⟶ Ω` plus axioms) lives separately in `HeytingLean.Topos.LTfromNucleus`.
cells.overlay_summary: Universal closure operator on subobjects (Lawvere–Tierney modality) specified by local closures stable under pullback.

cells.signature: {C : Type u} → [inst : CategoryTheory.Category C] → CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C → C → Type (max u v)
cells.docstring: The type of `J`-closed sieves on `X`, presented as the range of the sieve nucleus.
cells.overlay_summary: The type of `J`-closed sieves on `X`, presented as the range of the sieve nucleus.

cells.docstring: Packages the re-entry operation as a nucleus on a primary algebra together with the minimal pair of complementary fixed points generated by the primordial distinction.
cells.overlay_summary: Formalizes Spencer-Brown's concept of re-entry (self-referential forms) using the mathematical machinery of nuclei from pointfree topology. The structure captures a nucleus on a primary algebra with two distinguished orthogonal fixed points representing the primordial distinction.

cells.docstring: Labeled multi-step paths, as a `Type` (so distinct label sequences are distinct morphisms).
cells.overlay_summary: Labeled multi-step paths, as a `Type` (so distinct label sequences are distinct morphisms).

cells.signature: {a b : HeytingLean.LoF.Combinators.Category.MWObj} → HeytingLean.LoF.Combinators.Category.instCategoryMWObj.Hom a b → HeytingLean.LoF.Combinators.Category.instCategoryMWObj.Hom a b → Type
cells.docstring: Non-thin 2-cells between parallel paths: paths in the completion-rule generator quiver.
cells.overlay_summary: Non-thin 2-cells between parallel paths: paths in the completion-rule generator quiver.

cells.signature: structure Model where R : Reentry α
cells.docstring: Clifford bridge model carrying pairs of α.
cells.overlay_summary: The Clifford bridge model, which lifts a reentry nucleus to operate on pairs. Provides encode/decode functions to move between the Omega fixed points and the pair carrier.

cells.signature: structure Model where R : Reentry α
cells.docstring: Graph bridge model: adjacency inherits the order on α.
cells.overlay_summary: A graph bridge model wrapping a re-entry nucleus R. The ambient type α serves as the graph carrier, with adjacency relation given by the order ≤. This bridges Laws of Form structure to graph-theoretic semantics.

cells.signature: structure DialParam where dimension : ℕ; dial : Dial α
cells.docstring: Annotates breathing data with ladder dimension.
cells.overlay_summary: Keeps track of which ladder stage a breathing profile belongs to.
Defines/computes Right Inverse At. Use to construct or compute the corresponding value in proofs or definitions.

cells.docstring: Tensor bridge data: dimension together with the core nucleus.
cells.overlay_summary: Tensor bridge data: dimension together with the core nucleus.

cells.docstring: Replacement/repair selectors: admissible maps `B → H(A,B)` with their admissibility proof.
cells.overlay_summary: Replacement/repair selectors: admissible maps `B → H(A,B)` with their admissibility proof.

cells.signature: structure IntReentry (α : Type u) [PrimaryAlgebra α] where nucleus : IntNucleus α
cells.docstring: Interior-style re-entry packaging.
cells.overlay_summary: A wrapper structure that packages an IntNucleus for convenient use. Provides CoeFun instance so the reentry can be applied directly as a function.

cells.docstring: A (single-shot) protocol in the real world, attempting to realize an ideal functionality.
cells.overlay_summary: A (single-shot) protocol in the real world, attempting to realize an ideal functionality.

cells.docstring: Clifford projector model couples the legacy bridge with a target projector carrier.
cells.overlay_summary: The Clifford projector model couples the legacy bridge with a target projector carrier. It is a fundamental concept in the study of projective geometry and algebraic structures.

cells.signature: {C : Type u_1} → [inst : PartialOrder C] → HeytingLean.LoF.CryptoSheaf.PosetSite C → Type (max (max u_1 u_2) (u_3 + 1))
cells.docstring: A presheaf of computations over a poset site.
cells.overlay_summary: A presheaf of computations over a poset site.

cells.signature: {α : Type u} → [inst : HeytingLean.LoF.PrimaryAlgebra α] → HeytingLean.LoF.Reentry α → Type (max u (v + 1))
cells.docstring: Uniform interface bundling a bridge carrier together with its certified round-trip.
cells.overlay_summary: Packages a bridge carrier plus a certified encode/decode pair so cryptographic transports satisfy the usual round-trip law.
